name: cleanup_phase5_break_monoliths
outcome_goal: Split 3 monolithic files into focused packages: cli/main.py, workflow_api.py, CommitmentMap.tsx

jobs.label: p5a_split_cli_main
jobs.prompt:
Split the monolithic cli/main.py (3,021 lines) into a commands/ package.

You are working in /Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow.

Current state: surfaces/cli/main.py is 3000+ lines handling ALL CLI commands.

Target:
surfaces/cli/
  main.py              (slim entry: arg parsing + command dispatch, ~200 lines)
  commands/
    __init__.py
    dispatch.py      (dispatch/run/status/wait commands)
    query.py         (query/search/recall commands)
    operate.py       (health/operator/friction commands)
    admin.py         (migrate/reindex/validate commands)

Steps:
1. Read surfaces/cli/main.py in full.
2. Group commands by domain (dispatch, query, operate, admin).
3. Create commands/ package with __init__.py.
4. Extract each command group into its own module with handler functions.
5. Slim main.py to: argparse definition + import handlers + dispatch to handler.
6. Update any imports of surfaces.cli.main elsewhere.

Preserve all existing CLI behavior exactly. Same commands, same args, same output.

Verify: PYTHONPATH='.' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c "from surfaces.cli.main import main; print('OK')"

jobs.label: p5b_split_workflow_api
jobs.prompt:
Split the monolithic workflow_api.py (2,882 lines) into a handlers/ package.

You are working in /Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow.

Current state: surfaces/api/workflow_api.py is 2800+ lines handling all dispatch REST endpoints.

Target:
surfaces/api/
  workflow_api.py      (slim router: mounts sub-routers, ~100 lines)
  handlers/
    __init__.py
    workflow_run.py    (run/wait/status/cancel endpoints)
    workflow_query.py  (search/receipts/artifacts/list endpoints)
    workflow_notify.py (notifications/events/streaming endpoints)
    workflow_admin.py  (validate/health/inspect endpoints)

Steps:
1. Read surfaces/api/workflow_api.py in full.
2. Identify all route handlers and group by domain.
3. Create handlers/ package.
4. Extract each group into its module.
5. Slim workflow_api.py to: create router + import and mount sub-routers from handlers/.
6. Update any imports elsewhere that reference functions from workflow_api.

Preserve all API behavior exactly. Same routes, same responses.

Verify: PYTHONPATH='.' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c "from surfaces.api.workflow_api import *; print('OK')"

jobs.label: p5c_split_commitment_map
jobs.prompt:
Split CommitmentMap.tsx (1,596 lines) into focused sub-components.

You are working in /Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow.

Current state: surfaces/app/src/canvas/CommitmentMap.tsx is 1596 lines handling canvas rendering, node layout, edge drawing, selection, and interaction all in one file.

Target:
surfaces/app/src/canvas/
  CommitmentMap.tsx       (slim orchestrator: state + composition, ~300 lines)
  CanvasContainer.tsx     (viewport, zoom, pan)
  CanvasContainer.css
  NodeRenderer.tsx        (renders individual nodes/cards)
  NodeRenderer.css
  EdgeLayer.tsx           (SVG edge drawing between nodes)
  SelectionHandler.tsx    (click/drag selection logic)

Steps:
1. Read CommitmentMap.tsx and CommitmentMap.css in full.
2. Identify logical boundaries: canvas container, node rendering, edge drawing, selection.
3. Extract each concern into its own component.
4. Keep CommitmentMap.tsx as orchestrator composing sub-components.
5. Split CSS accordingly.
6. Type all props with TypeScript interfaces.

Preserve all visual behavior exactly.

Verify: cd 'surfaces/app' && npx tsc --noEmit
